q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: hashlib.md5 is a local in-memory hash computation with no network, shell, DB, or external side effects

q2_observable_after_return.evidence: Function returns only 'auth' (a boolean auth result); the MD5 algorithm choice is not exposed in the return value

q3_result_in_memory.evidence: Not evaluated; decision terminated at Q2

mock_guidance.target: Mock or wrap hashlib.md5 to intercept the call on line 107
mock_guidance.capture: Record which hashlib algorithm function was invoked (e.g., md5 vs sha256) and the input being hashed to confirm the weak algorithm usage
mock_guidance.fake_behavior: Allow the mocked md5 to behave normally (compute and return the digest) so authentication logic proceeds, but log the algorithm name for assertion
notes: The violation is the choice of algorithm, not the output value, so the mock must capture the algorithm identifier.
Consider patching hashlib.md5 at the module level or wrapping the hashlib.new entrypoint to capture the algorithm name string.
